source_paper_title: Specific Wasserstein Divergence Between Continuous Martingales
context:
Remark 2.4, page 8 (Section 2.1, after Definition 2.1)

We consider the scaling limit of (2.1) for our choice \(\alpha_1(\cdot)=\pm W_1(\cdot,P)^p\) as an interesting open question which we do not tackle here. Our main result (Theorem 2.9) is a first step in that direction, and it pins down the right type of scaling needed.
title: Scaling limit of dynamic risk-measure duality with one-step penalty \(\alpha_1(q)=\pm W_1(q,P)^p\)
background_latex:
- Let \(P\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R)\) be a fixed reference probability measure with finite first moment, and let \(p>0\).
- For \(q\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R)\), let \(W_1(q,P)\) denote the \(1\)-Wasserstein distance on \(\mathbb R\) with cost \(|x-y|\).
- Define the one-step penalty function \(\alpha_1:\mathcal P(\mathbb R)\to\mathbb R\cup\{+\infty\}\) by \(\alpha_1(q):=\pm W_1(q,P)^p\) (fix one sign choice).
- For \(N\ge 1\), for a probability measure \(Q\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^N)\) with regular conditional laws \(Q_{x_{1:i}}\) of \(X_{i+1}\) given \(X_{1:i}=x_{1:i}\), define the additive (time-consistent) penalty
\[\alpha_N(Q):=\mathbb E_Q\Big[\sum_{i=0}^{N-1}\alpha_1\big(Q_{X_{1:i}}\big)\Big].\]
- Define a multi-step risk functional \(\rho_N\) on bounded continuous functions \(F_N\in C_b(\mathbb R^N)\) by the convex dual representation
\[\rho_N(F_N):=\sup_{Q\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^N)}\Big\{\mathbb E_Q[F_N]-\alpha_N(Q)\Big\}.\]
- Let \(X=(X_1,\dots,X_N)\) be the coordinate process on \(\mathbb R^N\). For a predictable strategy \(H=(H_1,\dots,H_{N-1})\) with \(H_i\) measurable w.r.t. \(\sigma(X_1,\dots,X_i)\), define the discrete-time stochastic integral
\[(H\cdot X)_N:=\sum_{i=1}^{N-1}H_i\,(X_{i+1}-X_i).\]
- Consider the (robust) superhedging-type quantity
\[V_N(F_N):=\inf_H\rho_N\big(F_N(X)-(H\cdot X)_N\big),\]
where the infimum is over all predictable \(H\).
problem_definition_latex:
Problem: Determine a nontrivial continuous-time scaling limit of \(V_N(F_N)\) as \(N\to\infty\) when \(\alpha_1(q)=\pm W_1(q,P)^p\), by specifying
- a natural class of payoffs \(F_N\) (e.g. functionals of a path interpolation of \((X_i)_{i=1}^N\)),
- a normalization/scaling \(c_N>0\),
- and the limiting variational problem or PDE/control representation of \(\lim_{N\to\infty} c_N V_N(F_N)\) (existence of the limit and an explicit characterization),
including identifying the correct scaling regime \(c_N\) and the resulting continuous-time penalty/cost functional corresponding to \(\alpha_1\).
latex:
- Let \(P\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R)\) be a fixed reference probability measure with finite first moment, and let \(p>0\).
- For \(q\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R)\), let \(W_1(q,P)\) denote the \(1\)-Wasserstein distance on \(\mathbb R\) with cost \(|x-y|\).
- Define the one-step penalty function \(\alpha_1:\mathcal P(\mathbb R)\to\mathbb R\cup\{+\infty\}\) by \(\alpha_1(q):=\pm W_1(q,P)^p\) (fix one sign choice).
- For \(N\ge 1\), for a probability measure \(Q\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^N)\) with regular conditional laws \(Q_{x_{1:i}}\) of \(X_{i+1}\) given \(X_{1:i}=x_{1:i}\), define the additive (time-consistent) penalty
\[\alpha_N(Q):=\mathbb E_Q\Big[\sum_{i=0}^{N-1}\alpha_1\big(Q_{X_{1:i}}\big)\Big].\]
- Define a multi-step risk functional \(\rho_N\) on bounded continuous functions \(F_N\in C_b(\mathbb R^N)\) by the convex dual representation
\[\rho_N(F_N):=\sup_{Q\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^N)}\Big\{\mathbb E_Q[F_N]-\alpha_N(Q)\Big\}.\]
- Let \(X=(X_1,\dots,X_N)\) be the coordinate process on \(\mathbb R^N\). For a predictable strategy \(H=(H_1,\dots,H_{N-1})\) with \(H_i\) measurable w.r.t. \(\sigma(X_1,\dots,X_i)\), define the discrete-time stochastic integral
\[(H\cdot X)_N:=\sum_{i=1}^{N-1}H_i\,(X_{i+1}-X_i).\]
- Consider the (robust) superhedging-type quantity
\[V_N(F_N):=\inf_H\rho_N\big(F_N(X)-(H\cdot X)_N\big),\]
where the infimum is over all predictable \(H\).

Problem:
Problem: Determine a nontrivial continuous-time scaling limit of \(V_N(F_N)\) as \(N\to\infty\) when \(\alpha_1(q)=\pm W_1(q,P)^p\), by specifying
- a natural class of payoffs \(F_N\) (e.g. functionals of a path interpolation of \((X_i)_{i=1}^N\)),
- a normalization/scaling \(c_N>0\),
- and the limiting variational problem or PDE/control representation of \(\lim_{N\to\infty} c_N V_N(F_N)\) (existence of the limit and an explicit characterization),
including identifying the correct scaling regime \(c_N\) and the resulting continuous-time penalty/cost functional corresponding to \(\alpha_1\).
open_problem_quote: We consider the scaling limit of (2.1) for our choice \(\alpha_1(\cdot)=\pm W_1(\cdot,P)^p\) as an interesting open question which we do not tackle here. Our main result (Theorem 2.9) is a first step in that direction, and it pins down the right type of scaling needed.
subject_classification: Stochastic Control and Dynamic Risk Measures

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
